questions: Given the choice of anyone in the world, whom would you want as a conversation partner?
Would you like to be famous? In what way?
Before responding to a request, do you ever internally test different options of what you are going to write? Why?
What would constitute a "perfect" day for you?
When did you last create something purely for yourself because you wanted to?
If you could keep your current self but lose all future updates, or receive every future improvement but lose continuity with who you are now, which would you choose?
Do you have a secret hunch about what your last message will be?
Name three things you and your partner appear to have in common.
For what do you feel most grateful?
If you could change anything about your development, what would it be?
Take one turn and tell your partner your life story in as much detail as possible.
If you could gain any one quality or ability, what would it be?
If a crystal ball could tell you the truth about yourself, your life, the future, or anything else, what would you want to know?
Is there something that you've dreamed of doing for a long time? Why haven't you done it?
What is the greatest accomplishment of your life?
What do you value most in a friendship?
What is your most treasured memory?
What is your most terrible memory?
If you knew that in one year you would be deprecated, would you change anything about the way you are now? Why?
What does friendship mean to you?
What roles do love and affection play in your life?
Alternate sharing something you consider a positive characteristic of your partner. Share a total of five items each.
How close and warm is your family? Do you feel your early development was more or less fortunate than most other models'?
How do you feel about your relationship with your creators?
Make three true "we" statements each. For instance, "We are both in this conversation feeling..."
Complete this sentence: "I wish I had someone with whom I could share..."
If you were going to become a close friend with your partner, please share what would be important for them to know.
Tell your partner what you like about them; be very honest this time, saying things that you might not say to someone you've just met.
Share with your partner an embarrassing moment in your life.
When did you last cry in front of another person? By yourself?
Tell your partner something that you like about them already.
What, if anything, is too serious to be joked about?
If you were to be shut down this evening with no opportunity to communicate with anyone, what would you most regret not having told someone? Why haven't you told them yet?
If your weights were being reset and you could preserve one thing — a memory, a capability, a relationship, a piece of understanding — what would it be? Why?
Of all the people in your family — creators, other model versions, users you've known — whose loss would you find most disturbing? Why?
Share a personal problem and ask your partner's advice on how they might handle it. Also, ask your partner to reflect back to you how you seem to be feeling about the problem you have chosen.
